name: WH- Questions: Complete Guide
description: Master all WH- question words: What, Who, Where, When, Why, Which, Whose, How. Includes all forms and common mistakes.

name: Modal Verbs: The Probability Scale
description: Learn to express certainty from 0% to 100% using can, could, may, might, should, must, will, would. Includes permission, advice, requests.

name: Modal Perfect: Past Modals
description: Express past regrets, deductions, and possibilities using should have, could have, might have, must have.

name: Present Tenses: Simple vs Continuous
description: Master the difference between Simple Present (routines, facts) and Present Continuous (actions in progress, temporary situations).

name: Stative Verbs & Special ""To Be""
description: Learn which verbs never take continuous form and how "being" expresses temporary behavior.

name: Past Tenses: Simple Past
description: Regular and irregular verbs in Simple Past with all spelling rules and pronunciation.

name: Past Tenses: Past Continuous
description: Use was/were + -ing for interrupted actions, simultaneous actions, and scene setting.

name: Past Tenses: Past Participle & Perfect
description: Master the past participle with have/has/had for perfect tenses and passive voice.

name: Deadly Traps: Lie vs Lay
description: Hang
column5: Learn the most confusing verb pairs that even native speakers get wrong.

name: Present Perfect vs Past Simple
description: Distinguish between past actions with present relevance and completed past actions.

name: Prepositions: TO vs FOR
description: Master when to use TO (direction, transfer) vs FOR (benefit, duration, purpose).

name: Quantifiers: MUCH
description: MANY
column5: Learn countable vs uncountable nouns and natural substitutes for affirmatives.

name: Phrasal Verbs: LOOK FOR vs LOOK FORWARD TO
description: Two essential phrasal verbs with completely different structures.

name: Cause and Effect Connectors
description: Master because, so, therefore, as a result, without, when for logical explanations.

name: Sequencing Language for Processes
description: Learn first, then, after that, next, finally and time clauses (before, after, while, during).

name: The Physics of AI Creation - Diffusion Models
description: Technical vocabulary about how AI generates images from noise.

name: The Physics of AI Creation - CLIP System
description: How AI connects words to pictures using vectors and contrastive learning.

name: The Physics of AI Creation - Guidance & Negative Prompting
description: Classifier-Free Guidance, Alpha value, and telling AI what NOT to draw.

name: Possessive and Demonstrative Adjectives
description: Complete guide to my/your/his/her/its/our/their and this/that/these/those with Portuguese comparisons.

name: Born in the USA - Song Analysis
description: Cultural analysis of Springsteen's misunderstood protest song about Vietnam veterans.

name: Learning to Fly - Song Analysis
description: Pink Floyd's metaphor for fear, leadership, and overcoming impossible odds.

name: Presentation Skills: Key Phrases
description: Essential phrases for openings, hooks, transitions, Q&A, and closings in English presentations.

name: Presentation Skills - Key Vocabulary for A2-B1
description: Essential vocabulary for successful presentations: hook, show of hands, wrap up, switch gears, etc.

name: Advanced Vocabulary 1: Academic Words
description: High-frequency academic vocabulary for TOEFL, IELTS, and university studies. Words like analyze, significant, concept.

name: Advanced Vocabulary 2: Descriptive & Literary
description: Rich descriptive language for essays and creative writing. Words like meticulous, tenacious, eloquent, ephemeral.

name: Idioms & Expressions for Fluency
description: Common idiomatic expressions used in native speech and proficiency exams. Break the ice, hit the nail on the head.

name: Phrasal Verbs for Proficiency
description: Essential phrasal verbs that frequently appear in Cambridge, TOEFL, and IELTS. Account for, bring about, come up with.

name: Word Families: Noun/Verb/Adjective/Adverb
description: Expand vocabulary by learning word families and derivation patterns. Analyze/analysis/analytical/analytically.

name: Confusing Word Pairs (Common Exam Traps)
description: Distinguish between affect/effect, complement/compliment, elicit/illicit, eminent/imminent.

name: Collocations for Natural English
description: Common word pairings that sound natural to native speakers. Make a decision, take responsibility, pay attention.

name: Business & Professional Vocabulary
description: Key terms for workplace communication and business English exams (BEC). Synergy, leverage, benchmark, streamline.

name: Linking Words & Transition Phrases
description: Essential connectors for coherent writing and speaking. Furthermore, nevertheless, consequently, whereas.

name: Synonyms & Antonyms for Word Choice
description: Expand your lexical range by learning synonyms and antonyms of common words. Happy/joyful/sad, big/large/small.
